data.content_ref: inline:contradicts: The 'network epidemic of institutional despair' (3cd717da) and the 'synchronous pandemic of hemorrhagic fever' (fb7a02bb) both model collapse as a contagion spreading along palatial networks, but they posit mutually exclusive transmission substrates—psychological despair versus a biological pathogen. They are refuted if the archaeological record contains evidence of mass mortality consistent with a biological agent (which would support the hemorrhagic fever model over the despair model) or, conversely, if it lacks such evidence and instead shows a continuity of human remains without disease markers (which would eliminate the biological substrate and favor the psychological one).
